classification.layer2-runtime: Automatically active when the upstream capability and Android have a necessary operational intersection.
classification.layer2-capability: Layer 2 exposes the neutral engine or bridge; Layer 3 owns optional UI, preference, or product policy.
classification.native-already: The public upstream capability works in System WebView without an Android-specific adapter.
classification.not-applicable: The capability has no necessary intersection with the selected native-PTY Android host.
classification.experimental: The upstream capability is not part of the Layer 2 completion gate until upstream stabilizes it.

core_capabilities.android_boundary: Raw PTY bytes enter Terminal.write; parsing, buffers, modes, cursor, selection, scrollback, reflow, mouse protocol, and rendering remain upstream-owned.
WebView/xterm input is transported byte-preservingly to the service-owned PTY.
core_capabilities.layer3_boundary: Custom key actions may call the public Layer 2 input capability later; Layer 2 does not reinterpret ordinary keys.

core_capabilities.android_boundary: One in-flight output batch, ordered sequence ACK, and bounded replay/snapshot state.
Android layout, insets, rotation, IME viewport, ResizeObserver, and visualViewport converge on deduplicated TIOCSWINSZ.
core_capabilities.layer3_boundary: Optional layout chrome must request refit through the public Layer 2 capability.

core_capabilities.android_boundary: WebView remains IME and key authority; Android reports physical-keyboard state without duplicate key translation.
core_capabilities.layer3_boundary: Optional modifier bars and special keys are Layer 3.

core_capabilities.android_boundary: Bounded explicit copy/paste operations use Android ClipboardManager.
core_capabilities.layer3_boundary: Clipboard buttons or gestures are Layer 3.

core_capabilities.android_boundary: Official ClipboardAddon must use an Android-backed IClipboardProvider.
core_capabilities.layer3_boundary: Permission prompts or clipboard UX are Layer 3; protocol semantics remain upstream.

core_capabilities.android_boundary: Validated HTTP/HTTPS activation is routed to ACTION_VIEW.
core_capabilities.layer3_boundary: Link history, previews, menus, and browser UI are Layer 3.

core_capabilities.android_boundary: Rate-limited Android haptic feedback provides a neutral host signal.
core_capabilities.layer3_boundary: User-selectable sound, vibration pattern, or enablement policy is Layer 3.

core_capabilities.android_boundary: Layer 2 must retain the current OSC 0/2 title as neutral session state.
core_capabilities.layer3_boundary: Displaying the title in tabs, toolbar, notification, or task label is Layer 3.

core_capabilities.authority: Android Configuration.uiMode and xterm public theme option
core_capabilities.android_boundary: Layer 2 reports light/dark state through the public customization capability but does not define a terminal palette.
core_capabilities.layer3_boundary: Actual theme objects and user theme preferences are Layer 3.

core_capabilities.android_boundary: Android accessibility and touch-exploration state control screenReaderMode.
core_capabilities.layer3_boundary: Product-specific accessibility UI is Layer 3; localization of upstream strings remains a pending Layer 2 Android-resource bridge.

core_capabilities.android_boundary: Android locale resources must supply upstream promptLabel and tooMuchOutput strings.
core_capabilities.layer3_boundary: Product copy outside the upstream terminal strings is Layer 3.

core_capabilities.android_boundary: Android fontScale multiplies each terminal instance's captured upstream default and refits geometry without compounding.
core_capabilities.layer3_boundary: Font family, explicit font size preference, line height, and letter spacing are Layer 3.

core_capabilities.android_boundary: Only truthful cell, pixel, rows/columns, title, refresh, and bounded resize reports may be enabled; desktop move/stack/iconify operations remain inapplicable.
core_capabilities.layer3_boundary: Fullscreen or product window-management UI is Layer 3.

core_capabilities.authority: loadAddon, parser, buffer, markers, decorations, link providers, key/wheel handlers
core_capabilities.android_boundary: Layer 2 does not wrap public APIs that require no Android operation and never uses xterm private APIs.
core_capabilities.layer3_boundary: Layer 3 may consume public xterm APIs only through the stable Layer 2 customization surface.

core_capabilities.authority: Android Service/WebView lifecycle plus xterm write/serialize APIs
core_capabilities.android_boundary: Service-owned PTY survives Activity/WebView replacement; serialized state and bounded raw tail restore the frontend.
core_capabilities.layer3_boundary: Session tabs, persistence, and workspace management are Layer 3.

official_addons.android_boundary: The selected backend is a native service-owned PTY, not a WebSocket process transport.
official_addons.layer3_boundary: A future remote/WebSocket session product is Layer 3.

official_addons.android_boundary: Use the official addon with an Android ClipboardManager-backed IClipboardProvider for OSC 52.
official_addons.layer3_boundary: Clipboard UI and user policy are Layer 3.

official_addons.android_boundary: Fits the WebView container and feeds bounded PTY geometry.
Use upstream defaults for SIXEL, IIP, partial Kitty graphics, storage, payload, pixel, and size-report behavior unless Android evidence requires a neutral safety bound.
official_addons.layer3_boundary: User-selectable protocol toggles and custom limits are Layer 3.

official_addons.android_boundary: Expose the official addon without selecting a font or ligature policy.
official_addons.layer3_boundary: Font and ligature enablement are Layer 3.

official_addons.android_boundary: Parse OSC 9;4 and expose neutral progress state through Layer 2.
official_addons.layer3_boundary: Toolbar, notification, tab badge, and display policy are Layer 3.

official_addons.android_boundary: Expose official buffer-search operations and result state.
official_addons.layer3_boundary: Search field, Ctrl+F, navigation controls, decorations, and preferences are Layer 3.

official_addons.android_boundary: Opaque snapshots plus output watermark and bounded raw tail support replacement frontends.
Upstream marks the addon experimental and not yet published to npm.
official_addons.layer3_boundary: May enter a separate experiment only after an explicit decision.

official_addons.android_boundary: Register the official Unicode 11 provider without selecting it as active.
Expose official font preload and relayout capability for local APK assets.
official_addons.layer3_boundary: Font assets, family choice, and fallback policy are Layer 3.

official_addons.android_boundary: Detected HTTP/HTTPS links use the same validated ACTION_VIEW bridge as OSC 8.
official_addons.layer3_boundary: Link UI, history, preview, and browser behavior are Layer 3.

official_addons.android_boundary: Attempt WebGL2 and permanently fall back to xterm core DOM rendering after activation failure or context loss.
official_addons.layer3_boundary: Renderer preference UI is Layer 3; Layer 2 must not require it.

webview_boundary.capability: local DOM, canvas, WebGL2, ResizeObserver, visualViewport, WebMessagePort, IME and accessibility primitives used by xterm.js
generic remote navigation, browser history, arbitrary file chooser, download manager, web authentication, page permissions and browser chrome
